Assumption: Power BI is the primary BI tool for implementing dashboards and visuals in the current project phase.
Evidence_Notes: User referenced PBIX files and Power BI dashboards in earlier prompts.

Assumption: A single unified project-level fact table (FactProjects) holding A+B fields is acceptable as the core data model.
Evidence_Notes: We have repeatedly structured schema A+B around project-level obligations and KPIs.

Assumption: The orchestration spec should support at least MCE (engineering consultancy) and Neex (multi-regulated financial group) in the same meta-architecture via entity profiles.
Evidence_Notes: User has repeatedly worked across MCE and Neex, and requested a unified master plan.

Assumption: Decision-making authority can be represented via levels L1–L4 plus board, and linked to EMP_SENIORITY_LEVEL and APPROVAL_DOA_LEVEL.
Evidence_Notes: We discussed DOA level concepts, but exact mapping to internal HR/authority structure is not confirmed.

Assumption: Each employee will have a single 'Agent Buddy' that covers all their project/department tasks rather than multiple specialized buddies per tool or entity.
Evidence_Notes: User requested a 'super smart Agent Buddy for each employee'; multiplicity per person not specified.

Assumption: Role-, department-, and DOA-based RBAC will be sufficient to gate RAG results and Buddy responses, without needing very fine-grained per-document policies initially.
Evidence_Notes: User requested seniority and hierarchical reporting-based filtering; level of granularity not fully defined.

Assumption: A generic Tasks/Events model with fields like TASK_TYPE, RELATED_PROJECT_UID, RELATED_EMP_UID, PRIORITY, SLA_DAYS, and STATUS is acceptable as the backbone for workflow and notifications.
Evidence_Notes: User consistently references task/alert/notification flows, but specific tool (e.g. ClickUp vs Jira vs ERPNext) is not fixed.

Assumption: YAML/Markdown/CSV/XLSX-based configuration files are acceptable as the control surface for MCP/orchestration and can later be migrated to a DB if needed.
Evidence_Notes: User repeatedly requested YAML/Markdown specs and CSV/XLSX outputs.

Assumption: Future multi-part executions should be hard-split across chat messages with explicit pauses, uncertainty logging, and HITL checkpoints before continuing.
Evidence_Notes: User explicitly requested multi-execution behavior with circuit-breaker and Uncertainty Log mechanism.
